tavily.answer: Recent developments in the West Bank have seen multiple clashes between Israelis and Palestinians, particularly in the town of Huwara following an alleged theft of sheep belonging to Israeli civilians. The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) reported incidents of stone throwing and the use of clubs between Israeli civilians and Palestinians. The IDF and Border Guard forces intervened to remove the civilians and the sheep from the village to prevent further friction. The Sha'a District Police have opened an investigation into the clashes. Meanwhile, the IDF has eased some closures on a West Bank Palestinian town but continues to block access to the main north-south highway, Route 60. In Gaza, Israel has closed border crossings, halting aid, amid tensions with Iran.
